target.name: Kelluu
target.founder: Janne Hietala, Jiri Jormakka, Jouni Lintu

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: NODA AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $125M

data.narrative:
Kelluu is a Series A defense and security company founded by Janne Hietala, Jiri Jormakka, and Jouni Lintu that has raised €20M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 8 private peers in the same sector and stage, supplemented by 2 public comps (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and reference to top-tier private peers including NODA AI, Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, and Saronic.

The peer-median method, the sole quantitatively applicable approach given available data, anchors analysis on the median valuation of $1.0B across the 8-company peer set. This universe spans a range from $125M to $3.0B, with illustrative comparables including Dream Security ($3.0B, Series B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B, Series B), Code Metal ($1.2B, Series B), and multiple peers at $1.0B valuation. The peer median reflects transactions and valuations contemporaneous with Kelluu's Series A positioning.

Triangulation across available methods yields a valuation range of $850M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $1.0B. This narrow band reflects reliance on a single method; key uncertainties include differentiation of Kelluu's technology relative to the peer set, capital efficiency relative to raised proceeds, and sector-specific demand dynamics in defense technology markets. Further primary diligence on revenue trajectory, customer concentration, and competitive moat would refine this range.
